name: Allkorn
notes: Non-profit network founded by Hans Larsson at SLU for participatory plant breeding and heritage grain revival; distributes 10 ears per variety to farmers annually

name: Gutekorn
notes: Six-farm cooperative on Gotland protecting ancient grains from the Ardre treasure cache; operates own gene bank with ~30 varieties

name: Brödinstitutet
notes: Swedish Bread Institute; knowledge hub for bread culture and heritage grains; promotes traditional Nordic bread varieties

name: Eldrimner
notes: National Centre for Small-Scale Artisan Food Processing; courses on sourdough and heritage grain baking; UNESCO Creative Cities partner

name: Hidden in Grains
notes: Gotland-based grain company working closely with Allkorn; sources ancient grains from Nordic farmers and mills heritage flour

name: Stangeland Mølle
notes: Historic Norwegian stone mill operating since 1846; uses 1927 German stone mill; processes five grain types from local Norwegian farmers

name: NORSØK Heritage Grain Project
notes: Norwegian Centre for Organic Agriculture; field trials comparing 25 old and modern spring wheat varieties for organic growing and artisan baking

name: Organic Denmark Grain Network
notes: Økologisk Landsforening grain section; promotes organic and heritage cereal cultivation across Denmark; works with Valsemøllen mills

name: Boreal Plant Breeding Heritage Program
notes: Finnish plant breeding company with heritage variety preservation program; breeds for extreme northern adaptation and winterhardiness

name: Helsinki Mills Heritage Project
notes: Family-owned since 1934; Finland's largest organic cereal producer; carbon-neutral milling from Finnish heritage grains

name: Heritage Grain Trust
notes: Founded by archaeobotanist John Letts; collects heritage grains from gene banks worldwide; supplies Oxford Artisan Distillery with ancient varieties

name: Scotland The Bread
notes: Not-for-profit cooperative owned by 300+ members; grows evolutionary wheat and rye on Balcaskie Estate; pioneered cyclone milling in UK

name: Brockwell Bake Association
notes: South London community growing heritage wheat since 2008; partners with organic farms in Sussex and Kent; promotes fresh-milled flour

name: Hodmedod's
notes: British grain and pulse revival company; grew UK's first commercial lentil crop in 2017; BBC Food & Farming Award winner 2017

name: Wakelyns Agroforestry
notes: Pioneering agroforestry research farm; created YQ population wheat with Organic Research Centre; 50+ acres of agroforestry

name: Real Bread Campaign
notes: Project of Sustain alliance; supports eight regional grain networks across UK; Real Bread For All community workshops

name: The Bread Lab
notes: WSU research lab founded by Dr Stephen Jones; 8000+ variety seed library; created Skagit 1109 whole-wheat variety; partners with top artisan bakers

name: Maine Grain Alliance
notes: Runs annual Kneading Conference drawing farmers millers and bakers from across US; followed by Maine Artisan Bread Fair with 3000+ attendees

name: Northern Plains Sustainable Agriculture Society
notes: Promotes sustainable grain farming across 17 states; education research and advocacy for organic and heritage grain in Northern Plains

name: Carolina Ground
notes: Micro flour mill founded by Jennifer Lapidus; grinds 500-1000 kg grain daily on Austrian stone mill; sources from local organic farms

name: Grist & Toll
notes: LA's first urban flour mill; founded by Nan Kohler; specializes in California-grown heritage Sonora wheat; Austrian wooden stone mill

name: Ironwood Organics
notes: Eastern Ontario organic farm growing heritage grains; supports local baking and brewing community; nutrient-dense grain focus

name: BC Grain Producers Association
notes: Provincial grain producers association; promotes local grain economy in BC; works with heritage grain farmers like Dan Jason of Salt Spring Seeds

name: Réseau Semences Paysannes
notes: French farmers' seed network with 70+ member organizations and ~40 Seed Houses across France; promotes peasant seed sovereignty and dynamic on-farm management

name: Association Kokopelli
notes: French association promoting heirloom seeds; 1600-2000 references including grains; works with 30 certified organic seed producers; branches in 6 countries

name: Les Maîtres de Mon Moulin
notes: Restored windmill in Cucugnan by Roland Feuillas; sources ancient grains from ~12 organic farmers; over 500 people trained in heritage grain baking
source: Les Maîtres de Mon Moulin official

name: IG Saatgut
notes: Interest group for GE-free seed work; European network of seed conservation organizations and breeders; advocates against seed patents

name: Saat:gut e.V.
notes: Organic open-pollinated seed breeding initiative backed by Bioland; breeds varieties for organic farming; works with Bingenheimer Saatgut AG

name: Arche Noah
notes: Austrian association preserving crop diversity; 5500 accessions including rare cereals; 800+ varieties of beans; network of hundreds of private seed savers

name: ProSpecieRara
notes: Swiss foundation for heritage crop and livestock preservation; 1250+ maintained varieties; network of 650 volunteer seed savers; initiated seed ordinance reform

name: Latvian Permaculture Association Seed Savers
notes: Latvian heritage plant network with 150+ identified local varieties; coordinates with Baltic seed savers in Estonia Lithuania and Denmark via NORDPLUS project
source: Latvian Permaculture Association; Growing Seed Savers

name: Jõgeva Plant Breeding Gene Bank
notes: Estonian crop gene bank with oldest accessions dating to 1875 (Sangaste rye); stores seeds at -18C per FAO standards; deposits to Svalbard Global Seed Vault
source: Estonian Crop Research Institute official; NordGen

name: Gamtines Žemdirbystes Institutas
notes: Lithuanian Natural Agriculture Institute; seed exchanges and heritage plant preservation; part of Baltic Growing Seed Savers network
source: Growing Seed Savers; FAO Lithuania report

name: Ölverk Pizza & Brewery
notes: Family-owned geothermally powered brewery in Hveragerði; brews with Icelandic-grown barley using geothermal steam; part of Icelandic grain revival

name: Faroese Grain Revival (CEREAL NPA)
notes: Interreg NPA CEREAL project reviving barley cultivation in Faroe Islands; ancient Tampar and Sigur barley varieties; 300kg harvest used by Føroya Bjór brewery

name: Flatbread Society
notes: Oslo-based art and food sovereignty project; community grain field on Losæter urban farm; visited VIR Vavilov Institute for seed heritage research

name: VIR Friends of Vavilov Heritage Network
notes: Community connected to N.I. Vavilov All-Russian Institute of Plant Genetic Resources; world's oldest and largest seed bank with 330000+ accessions spanning 2169 species
source: VIR official; Atlas Obscura; Crop Trust

name: Salt Spring Seeds
notes: Dan Jason's heritage seed farm; 30+ years collecting and testing 40+ traditional grain varieties from around the world; major Pacific Northwest seed source
source: Salt Spring Seeds catalog; BC Farms & Food
